title: At 22, she’s running Boston for her mom — and a future without Alzheimer’s
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Ella Wright, age 22, will run the Boston Marathon to honor her mother, diagnosed with early-onset Alzheimer's.
Wright's participation aims to raise awareness and funds for Alzheimer's research and support affected families.
The Boston resident has lost several family members to Alzheimer's, motivating her advocacy through the marathon.

title: Plymouth officer charged with child rape suspended by POST Commission
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Plymouth Police Officer Samantha Pelrine was suspended by the POST Commission after child rape charges.
Pelrine and her husband Daniel Forand were accused of abusing a boy in their custody from 2018 to 2025.
The couple, who posted bail, is prohibited from contacting the alleged victim or children under 18.

title: After 7 e-bike incidents in 10 days, Mass. police chief warns of impending ‘tragedy’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Milford police reported seven e-bike incidents in 10 days, prompting safety warnings from Chief Robert Tusino.
A 15-year-old crashed an e-bike into a car in Milford, marking the latest incident; no injuries were reported.
Massachusetts law allows e-bikes under 750 watts to be treated like bicycles, with helmet use mandatory for all riders.

title: Bridgewater police warn of Tom Brady impersonation scam
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Bridgewater police issued a warning about a scam involving an impersonator posing as Tom Brady.
A family in Bridgewater was defrauded by sending gift cards to the scammer impersonating the former Patriots quarterback.
The Federal Trade Commission advises against sending money or gift cards to unfamiliar individuals to avoid scams.

title: This Boston Marathon runner is supporting children in their hardest times
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Maria Castaneda, 52, from Cambridge, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for The Children's Room charity.
The Children's Room supports children and families through grief with understanding and healing services.
Castaneda expresses gratitude to the Boston community and aims to give back through her marathon run.

title: Review and setlist: Lady Gaga’s Mayhem Ball brought the spectacle, but Boston brought the heart
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lady Gaga's 'Mayhem Ball' concert at Boston's TD Garden featured gothic theatrics and highlighted her latest album, 'Mayhem'.
Fans created a special tribute with coordinated signs for Gaga's 40th birthday, bringing her to tears during the performance.
The concert blended new tracks with classic hits, including 'Poker Face' and 'Bad Romance', in a nearly three-hour showcase.

title: Would you try the new couch-style seating on United? Here’s what readers said.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines will introduce Relax Row, a couch-style seating option, in 2027 for economy passengers.
In a Boston.com reader poll, 61% expressed excitement to try the new seating concept, while 15% opposed it.
Concerns about cost, space, and passenger behavior were raised by readers skeptical about the seating option.

title: This first grade teacher is running the Boston Marathon to support her students
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Siobhan Salani, a Weymouth first grade teacher, is running the Boston Marathon for the Corey C. Griffin Foundation.
The foundation provides significant support and opportunities to Salani's students at Saint John Paul II Catholic Academy.
Salani is part of the Corey C. Griffin Foundation marathon team, expressing gratitude for its impact on Boston children.

title: A Maine beach is among the best in the U.S. in 2026, according to Conde Nast Traveler
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Sand Beach in Acadia National Park, Maine, is listed among America's best beaches for 2026 by Conde Nast Traveler.
Sand Beach is described as a hidden spot with pale sand and wild rocks, set between granite cliffs on Mount Desert Island.
Conde Nast suggests staying at Under Canvas Acadia or The Asticou Hotel for visitors exploring the area.

title: 16-year-old arrested in Allston shooting
author: John Waller
bullets: A 16-year-old boy was arrested for a shooting in Allston that injured another juvenile on March 26.
The teen faces multiple charges, including assault with intent to murder and carrying a firearm without a license.
The victim, injured near 222 Brighton Avenue, was hospitalized with non-life-threatening injuries, according to Boston police.
